NOTES ON ILLUSTRATIONS

PAGE
JAMES MADISON -

Frontispiece

SIR HENRY CLINTON.-Reproduced from an old steel engraving
after a painting by Smart -

3
RODNEY TRIUMPHANT. FROM GILLRAY'S CARICATURE
UPON THE OCCASION OF SIR GEORGE RODNEY'S VICTORY
OVER ADMIRAL DE GRASSE IN THE WEST INDIES.-
Reproduced from the print in the New York Public Library
(Lenox Building). This signal victory of Rodney over de
Grasse was considered the most important battle of the cen-
tury, and as a reward for his services Rodney was raised
to the peerage. The print shows Rodney trampling on the
French flag while receiving de Grasse's sword. In the back-
ground are sailors bringing ashore the treasure-chests, while
above Rodney is seen the Viscount's coronet, which never
reached his head. - -

5
YALE COLLEGE AND CHAPEL, 1786.-Reproduced from an old
print in the library at Yale University, New Haven, Conn. 7

HENRY SEYMOUR CONWAY.-Reproduced
from a print in the
European Magazine, 1782. -

9

-

NORTH SIDE OF WALL STREET, NEW YORK, 1785--Redrawn
from an old print -

IO

LORD SHELBURNE.-Reproduced from an old steel engraving II

SOUTH CAROLINA CURRENCY.-From the original in the New
York Public Library (Lenox Building). - -

13
GENERAL CARLETON'S RETURN OF REFUGEES.-From The
Memorial History of New York City -

14

vii
